name: HMS P 512 (P 512)
events: The history of HMS P 512 is incomplete but as complete as we can currently get it obtaining data from U.K. and U.S. sources.
This page was last updated in January 2018.

19 Mar 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) and HMS P 514 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN) departed New London, Connecticut, USA for Halifax, Canada. They were escorted by HMCS Calgary (T/Lt. G. Lancaster, RCNR) and HMCS Charlottetown (T/Lt. J.W. Bonner, RCNR). (1)
21 Mar 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) arrived at Halifax. (2)
12 Jun 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) arrived at Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island.
1 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises near Pictou, Nova Scotia. (1)
2 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises near Pictou, Nova Scotia. (1)
5 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises near Pictou, Nova Scotia. (1)

7 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises near Pictou, Nova Scotia. (1)
8 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises near Pictou, Nova Scotia. (1)
9 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises near Pictou, Nova Scotia. Upon completion of the exercises she proceeded to Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island. (1)
12 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) departed Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island to participate in A/S exercises in the exercise area off Pictou, Nova Scotia. (1)
13 Oct 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises near Pictou, Nova Scotia. (1)

6 Dec 1942 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) arrived at Halifax, coming from Pictou, for a period of maintenance and repairs. (1)
13 Jan 1943 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) departed Halifax for the Philadelphia Navy Yard where she was to refit. (3)
20 Jan 1943 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.C. Ogle, DSC, RN) arrived at the Philadelphia Navy Yard. (3)
16 Apr 1943 With her refit completed, HMS P 512 (Lt. R.B. Foster, RN), departed the Philadelphia Navy Yard for New London, Connecticut. (3)
17 Apr 1943 With her refit completed, HMS P 512 (Lt. R.B. Foster, RN), arrived at New London, Connecticut from the Philadelphia Navy Yard.
27 Apr 1943 HMS P 512 (Lt. R.B. Foster, RN) departed New London, Connecticut for Bermuda.
2 May 1943 HMS P 512 (Lt. R.B. Foster, RN) arrived at Bermuda where she was to aid in A/S training. She was escorted in by USS SC-679. (1)
12 May 1943 HMS P 512 (Lt. R.B. Foster, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Bermuda. (1)

15 Jun 1943 HMS P 512 (Lt. R.B. Foster, RN), while en-route to sea for exercises, ran aground in Town Cut Channel, St. Georges, Bermuda. She came off after about half an hour and returned to Ordnance Island, Bermuda.

27 Jul 1944 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.A. Wingate, DSC, RN) proceeded to the Bermuda Dockyard to effect repairs to her defective forwards hydroplanes. (1)
24 Aug 1944 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.A. Wingate, DSC, RN) departed Bermuda for the Philadelphia Navy Yard. (3)
28 Aug 1944 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.A. Wingate, DSC, RN) arrived at the Philadelphia Navy Yard from Bermuda. (3)
6 Sep 1944 HMS P 512 (Lt. J.A. Wingate, DSC, RN) was decommissioned at the Philadelphia Navy Yard and returned to the USN. She was however no recommissioned by the USN but laid up in reserve. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
